draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer chunked metrics (cards showing individual stats) over dense single-view dashboards
facade.label: Dashboard cards
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward breaking information into scannable, task-focused cards rather than comprehensive overviews
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer consolidated views, full-picture dashboards, or narrative summaries over fragmented metric displays

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer chunked metrics (cards showing individual stats) over dense single-view dashboards
evidence.implication: User leans toward breaking information into scannable, task-focused cards rather than comprehensive overviews

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer low-contrast, dark-mode aesthetics that reduce visual fatigue during long work sessions
facade.label: Minimal dark interface
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark mode, muted color palettes, high contrast text, night-friendly designs for CRM dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: Consider bright/light backgrounds, colorful accents, high-key lighting, daytime-optimized interfaces

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent navigation sidebar paired with card-based layouts for viewing clients and projects
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure and content grouping (sidebar vs tabs; cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick navigation access and visual content chunking; likely to accept similar persistent-nav layouts
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers tab-based or list-based navigation; may favor dense information hierarchies or horizontal navigation models

facade.hypothesis: A friendly, chat-like voice will feel more approachable to solo freelancers than corporate-speak
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use casual language, contractions, and encourage a personal relationship with the tool. Messaging like 'Hey, you've got 3 invoices due' rather than 'Invoice status: 3 outstanding.'
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should maintain a more formal, business-focused voice. Users prefer clarity and professionalism over warmth. Messaging like 'Outstanding invoices: 3' or neutral UI copy.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a softer, more inviting interface aesthetic over cold minimalism—especially in areas where they spend time reviewing clients and projects
facade.label: Warm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: The product should lean into human-centric design with warmer color palettes, softer edges, and approachable typography to build trust and comfort in client relationships
facade.rejectImplies: The product should prioritize clean, cool-toned, modern minimalism—bright whites, cool grays, sharp contrast—to convey professionalism and efficiency

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM users prefer snappy, zero-lag interactions over smooth animations when managing time-sensitive client data
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: Users value speed and directness in UI feedback—clicks should feel immediate, no easing or transition delays on core actions like adding contacts, logging time, or marking tasks done
facade.rejectImplies: Users expect and enjoy motion in the interface—transitions, subtle animations, and fluidity make interactions feel more polished and less abrupt, even if slightly slower

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want data-heavy reporting and metrics vs. simple, narrative summaries—reveals if they need detailed analytics or quick status snapshots
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want data-heavy reporting and metrics vs. simple, narrative summaries—reveals if they need detailed analytics or quick status snapshots
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need chronological visibility of projects and deadlines over metric snapshots, to manage overlapping work streams
facade.axisTargeted: Information hierarchy: time-based narrative vs. status-based metrics
facade.label: Timeline view
facade.acceptImplies: User values temporal context and sequence awareness; next probe should test granularity (daily/weekly/monthly) or filtering by client
facade.rejectImplies: User deprioritizes chronological overview; next probe should test alternative structures like Kanban boards or client-grouped lists

draft.title: Freelance CRM Dashboard
draft.summary: A clean, mobile-first CRM interface for freelancers to track clients, projects, and invoices. Warm, professional aesthetic with intuitive navigation.
